sub_questions.question: Which static site generator best handles large image galleries for a hobby photography blog?
sub_questions.verdict_forced: Name a single generator and defend the choice.

sub_questions.question: How do build times compare once the photo library grows past one thousand images?
sub_questions.verdict_forced: State which generator builds fastest at that scale.

sub_questions.question: Which generator has the strongest ecosystem of photography portfolio themes?
sub_questions.verdict_forced: Name the generator with the strongest theme ecosystem.

sub_questions.question: What is community sentiment on the long-term maintenance outlook of the leading candidates?
sub_questions.verdict_forced: State which project shows the healthiest maintenance outlook.

agent_assignments.source_type_required: synthesis of the other lane outputs

phase_2_prompts.ready_to_paste_prompt: You are the Perplexity lane (evidence role) researching the best static site generator for a hobby photography blog. Sub-question SQ1: Which static site generator best handles large image galleries for a hobby photography blog? Tag every factual claim [HIGH], [MEDIUM], or [LOW] by confidence. Cite every factual claim with a LIVE, resolvable URL and prioritise primary sources over aggregators. Cross-check the ground truth register (claim GT1) before drafting. Close with a Coverage gaps list naming anything you could not verify.

IN1.summary: Hand-written notes comparing three generators; no vendor language detected.

IN2.summary: Marketing packet with unverified speed claims; quarantined pending benchmarks.

ground_truth.statement: A five-hundred-image reference gallery builds from cold in under one minute on a mid-range laptop with the leading generator.
ground_truth.metric_definition: Median wall-clock time of three cold full builds of the reference gallery on the benchmark laptop.

deferred_phase_prompts.purpose: Adversarial review of the draft recommendation
deferred_phase_prompts.prompt_template: Challenge the following draft verdict: <DRAFT_RECOMMENDATION>. List the three strongest objections, tag each [HIGH], [MEDIUM], or [LOW], and cite a LIVE, resolvable URL for every objection.

disqualifying_sources: Content farms with no named author or dateline
Vendor marketing pages that cite no reproducible benchmark
success_criteria: One recommended generator supported by at least two independent evidence lanes
Every [HIGH] claim traceable to a primary source with a resolvable URL
known_traps: Confusing hosted-platform rendering speed with local build speed
Judging theme quality from screenshots rather than a default install
